name: Sierra de Pina Loop from Barracas
name_original: La Hoz del Río Mijares — Circular desde Barracas
description: This difficult 29.6-mile mountain biking loop from Barracas offers dramatic landscapes, panoramic views, and significant elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Barracas offers diverse and dramatic landscapes in El Alto Palancia, Castellón. The region is characterized by significant altitude differences, extensive trail networks, and a mix of natural and historical features. Riders encounter varied terrain, from distinctive red dirt paths to areas with unique savin juniper vegetation, providing a challenging and rewarding experience. Best mountain bike trails around Barracas The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Pina Loop from Barracas, a 29.6 miles (47.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. This challenging loop provides dramatic landscapes and expansive vistas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Circular Ermita de Pradas and Caseríos de San Agustín Trail, a moderate 16.4 miles (26.4 km) path. This route features distinctive red dirt paths and passes by historical landmarks such as the Castle of Pradas. Local mountain bikers also love the Masadas Blancas – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Barracas, a 31.0 miles (49.9 km) trail leading through the scenic Ojos Negros Greenway, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Mountain biking around Barracas is defined by significant altitude differences, distinctive red dirt paths, and historical elements. The network offers options for various skill levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Barracas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Barracas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Barracas?
A: Barracas offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 60 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Barracas?
A: You can expect diverse and dramatic landscapes with significant altitude differences. Trails feature distinctive red dirt paths, expansive vistas, and areas with unique savin juniper vegetation. The region offers a mix of challenging climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Barracas?
A: Yes, Barracas has options for beginners. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least 3 easy mountain bike trails available, perfect for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931131800/sierra-de-pina-loop-from-barracas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Pina Loop from Barracas&lt;/a&gt;, a 47.6 km trail with significant elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Santa Bárbara – Monument in Pina de Montalgrao loop from Barracas&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1300 meters of ascent, offer demanding rides with rewarding views.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Barracas?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Barracas are loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931131800/sierra-de-pina-loop-from-barracas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Pina Loop from Barracas&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996638171/circular-ermita-de-pradas-and-caserios-de-san-agustin-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Ermita de Pradas and Caseríos de San Agustín Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while mountain biking in Barracas?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy wide panoramas from high points like Alto de las Barracas, the highest point in the Region of Valencia. Trails also pass through areas with distinctive red earth, and you might encounter features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1756156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red earth&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural landmarks along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, Barracas has a rich history. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996638171/circular-ermita-de-pradas-and-caserios-de-san-agustin-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Ermita de Pradas and Caseríos de San Agustín Trail&lt;/a&gt;, pass by historical landmarks such as the Castle of Pradas. The region is also part of the ancient Camino Real de Aragón and the historic Holy Grail Route. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2304606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Jérica&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Barracas?
A: Barracas is suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are typically mild enough for riding, though higher elevations may experience colder conditions.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Barracas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse and dramatic landscapes, the extensive trail networks, and the challenging yet rewarding experience of navigating the significant altitude differences and distinctive red dirt paths.

Q: Are there any routes that follow former railway lines?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33870424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masadas Blancas – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Barracas&lt;/a&gt; utilizes the scenic Ojos Negros Greenway, a former railway line adapted into a bike route. This offers a unique experience, allowing cyclists to glide through incredible scenery on a relatively flat and well-maintained path.

Q: Are there any facilities or picnic areas near the trails?
A: While specific facilities directly on every trail may vary, you can find amenities in Barracas village. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2614427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albentosa Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt; is a nearby option for a break, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palancar Station Campsite&lt;/a&gt; offers facilities for those looking to extend their stay.
tips: There is a hiking route called “Las Hoces del Mijares” that you can explore. It is approximately 10.24 km long and offers stunning views.
Small town in the mountains on the slopes of Mount Pina. Enjoy lunch in its little bars before continuing your route along the paths that start from it.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Pina Loop from Barracas?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Barracas. You should be able to find parking within the town, which is known as a destination for sports tourism. Look for suitable parking areas near the main access points to the trails.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Pina Loop from Barracas suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult. The terrain involves significant elevation changes, including challenging climbs to high altitudes like the Alto de las Barracas, and varied technical sections. It is best suited for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Sierra de Pina Loop?
faqs.answer: Given the high altitudes and potentially harsh climatic conditions, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cold temperatures and snow at higher elevations, making the ride more challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Centro BTT Alto Palancia. While specific permits for individual rides are generally not required, it's always a good idea to check the official Centro BTT Alto Palancia website for any specific regulations or recommendations regarding trail usage, especially for larger groups or events.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Sierra de Pina Loop?
faqs.answer: You can expect a challenging and varied terrain. The route features high altitudes and significant elevation changes, with extensive networks of tracks, trails, and technical descents. Some sections, especially towards peaks like Alto de las Barracas, may even require pushing your bike. The landscape is dramatic, with panoramic views and unique vegetation like savin juniper.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers astonishing landscapes with panoramic views of deep valleys, such as the Turia Valley. You'll encounter unique vegetation, including savin juniper, adapted to the harsh climate. Notable points of interest include The Gorge and the "Pino de las Tres Garras" (Pine of the Three Claws). The route also passes near the Alto de las Barracas, the highest point in the Region of Valencia.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Pina Loop from Barracas dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but they should always be kept under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes sections of several other trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Sierra de Pina, Circular las Rozas, Pista Ibañez Altos, Camino de Montán, and Pista Pico Aguila, among others, as you navigate the loop.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Barracas?
faqs.answer: Barracas is a local town and a known destination for sports tourism, so you should find basic amenities such as cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on the Sierra de Pina Loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack essentials for a challenging ride. This includes plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate clothing layers for varied weather conditions at altitude, sun protection, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for riding this loop?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for significant climbs and descents. The final section to Alto de las Barracas, if you choose to ascend it, can be particularly demanding and may require pushing your bike. The unique savin juniper landscapes are a highlight, so take time to appreciate them. Always check the weather forecast before heading out, as conditions can change rapidly at higher elevations.
